# openshift_pxe_server (Resource)

Deploys a PXE server on a bastion host via SSH. Installs dnsmasq (DHCP proxy + TFTP) and nginx (HTTP), extracts the kernel and initrd from the discovery ISO, and serves them to nodes on the provisioning network. Nodes boot, register with the Assisted Installer, and install automatically. No BMC license required. Requires unbonded NICs during boot.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bastion_host` (String) Hostname or IP of the bastion host.
- `bastion_ssh_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded SSH private key for the bastion host.

### Optional

- `bastion_port` (Number) SSH port on the bastion host.
- `bastion_user` (String) SSH user on the bastion host.
- `dhcp_range_end` (String) End of DHCP range for PXE clients, e.g. 10.0.1.200.
- `dhcp_range_start` (String) Start of DHCP range for PXE clients, e.g. 10.0.1.100. Leave empty to use proxy DHCP (no range allocation).
- `http_port` (Number) Port for the nginx HTTP server serving the ISO/kernel/initrd.
- `interface` (String) Network interface on the bastion to serve PXE on (provisioning network).
- `iso_path` (String) Path to a locally-built ISO on the bastion (use openshift_agent_iso.iso_path for Agent-Based).
- `iso_url` (String) URL of the discovery ISO (use openshift_cluster.discovery_iso_url for Assisted Installer).

### Read-Only

- `pxe_server_url` (String) Base URL of the PXE HTTP server, e.g. http://bastion.example.internal:8080.
